channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Why tech in Congress lags behind the modern world
item.description: On a typical day, you can’t turn on the news without hearing someone say that Congress is broken. The implication is that this dereliction explains why the institution is inert and unresponsive to the American people.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Families sue TikTok in France over teen suicides they say are linked to harmful content
item.description: CASSIS, France (AP) — In the moment when her world shattered three years ago, Stephanie Mistre found her 15-year-old daughter, Marie, lifeless in the bedroom where she died by suicide.
item.dc:creator: TOM NOUVIAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: New federal CIO onboard at OMB
item.description: Greg Barbaccia takes over for Clare Martorana as the government’s lead technology executive. The post New federal CIO onboard at OMB first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: People are hawking TikTok-loaded phones for thousands on eBay, Facebook
item.description: People are listing phones preloaded with TikTok for tens of thousands of dollars on eBay, Facebook marketplace and other online storefronts — though it is not clear if there are many buyers at those prices. TikTok was briefly unavailable to U.S.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Meta to spend as much as $65 billion on AI efforts in 2025
item.description: The company intends to use the funds to build a data center “so large that it would cover a significant part of Manhattan,” Zuckerberg said in a Facebook post.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: OpenAI’s new Operator agent can use the web for you
item.description: OpenAI is releasing a preview version of its first AI agent, Operator, which is specifically designed to use a web browser and can, for example, book a table at a restaurant for the user on its own. (An AI agent is a system that can be given a task and then work on it independently.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The Navy’s researchers come up with a robot that operates out of this world
item.description: "We're focused on geosynchronous Earth orbit, where the most valuable real estate in space," said Bernie Kelm. The post The Navy’s researchers come up with a robot that operates out of this world first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: A Pixel-inspired productivity upgrade for any Android device
item.description: My friend and fellow Android appreciator, allow me to shower you with two delightfully simultaneous truths from here in the land o’ Googley matters: I love using Google’s Pixel devices in part because of all the genuinely useful features they add into the Android equation.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Los líderes empresariales prevén centrar la inversión en innovación, eficiencia y resiliencia
item.description: En un contexto de continuada incertidumbre en el mercado global, los líderes empresariales parecen haber arrancado el año con una dosis extra de positividad respecto a las perspectivas de su organización.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Nearly 250 million children missed school last year because of extreme weather, UNICEF says
item.description: The United Nations Children's Fund says at least 242 million children in 85 countries had their schooling interrupted last year because of heatwaves, cyclones, flooding and other extreme weather.
item.dc:creator: Gerald Imray, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Palo Alto Networks releases QRNG API framework
item.description: Cybersecurity company Palo Alto Networks has released the Quantum Random Number Generator (QRNG) Open API framework, enabling users to prepare for quantum security threats.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump AI project reignites feud between Musk, Sam Altman
item.description: Tensions between technology leaders Elon Musk and OpenAI CEO Sam Altman were on full display this week after the Tesla CEO slammed the new artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure project, Stargate, which is being funded in part by Altman's company.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Race is on to finish San Jose hotel in time for major Nvidia conference
item.description: Developers hope to gallop to the finish line to complete a San Jose hotel in time for a big Nvidia conference downtown.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Seeking growth, UK competition regulator probes Apple and Google
item.description: Apple and Google now face regulatory probes in the ailing UK market, where the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) has confirmed it intends to investigate both company’s mobile ecosystems under new digital market laws.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Revolutionizing procurement: How AI drives efficiency & profitability
item.description: AI is set to transform business operations, with the complex area of procurement ripe for digital innovation that will drive tangible impact. Procurement plays a crucial role in organizational success because it directly impacts operational efficiency, cost-effectiveness, and customer satisfaction.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The sickening truth: Healthcare data breaches reach all-time high
item.description: Healthcare records for more than 184 million Americans were breached in 2024, but you can take steps to minimize that risk. Kurt the CyberGuy explains.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tips on overcoming the loss of cherished, personal belongings in disasters
item.description: Losing important sentimental belongings — those items that represented who you are — can be traumatic for those who go through disasters that destroy homes.
item.dc:creator: JULIA RUBIN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How to cope when disasters strip away photos, heirlooms and other pieces of the past
item.description: They’re the possessions that tell your story: the photos of old friends and relatives. The ring your mom left you. The hand-knit Christmas stockings. Your grandfather's secretary desk and the letters inside.
item.dc:creator: JULIA RUBIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pakistan's parliament passes bill with sweeping controls on social media
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Pakistan’s lower house of parliament on Thursday passed a controversial bill that will give the government sweeping controls on social media, including sending users to prison for spreading disinformation.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to delete Facebook, Instagram and Threads if you don't like Meta's changes
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Should I stay or should I go — from Meta's social media platforms? That's what some Facebook, Instagram and Threads users are wondering after Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg's announcement this month that the company is relaxing rules on harmful content such as hate speech and abandoning its
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: This flying motorcycle can take you from traffic to sky in minutes
item.description: The Skyrider X1 from Rictor claims to be the "first amphibious flying passenger motorcycle," and it combines land and air travel in one sleek design.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to use resource-based authorization in ASP.NET Core
item.description: When implementing authorization in ASP.NET Core, we can adopt a declarative or an imperative approach. In a declarative authorization, you would typically use the [Authorize] attribute to restrict access to the resource.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump pardons Silk Road creator Ross Ulbricht
item.description: President Donald Trump pardoned Ross Ulbricht, the creator of the drug marketplace Silk Road who is revered by many cryptocurrency enthusiasts and Libertarians. “I just called the mother of Ross William Ulbright to let her know,” Trump wrote in a post on Truth Social, misspelling Ulbricht’s name.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Musk clashes with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman over Trump-supported Stargate AI data center project
item.description: Elon Musk is clashing with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman over the Stargate artificial intelligence infrastructure project touted by President Donald Trump, the latest in a feud between the two tech billionaires that started on OpenAI's board and is now testing Musk's influence with the new president.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Meta denies platforms are forcing users to follow Trump, Vance
item.description: "Those accounts are managed by the White House so with a new administration, the content on those Pages changes," Andy Stone wrote.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Amazon to close 7 warehouses in the Canadian province of Quebec and eliminate 1,700 jobs
item.description: Online retailer Amazon said Wednesday that it's closing all seven of its warehouses in the Canadian province of Quebec in the next two months. The e-commerce giant said the move would provide “even more savings to our customers over the long run.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration shuts down White House Spanish-language page and social media
item.description: Within hours of President Donald Trump’s inauguration, the new administration took down the Spanish-language version of the official White House website. The site — currently https://www.whitehouse.gov/es/ — now gives users an “Error 404” message.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 5 best home inventory apps to protect your property in case of emergency
item.description: Home inventory and restoration apps help itemize and track every item in your home in the event you have to make an insurance claim after a natural disaster.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump pardons founder of Silk Road website
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump announced Tuesday that he had pardoned Ross Ulbricht, the founder of Silk Road, an underground website for selling drugs.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Auto-followed Trump on Facebook? It's probably because you previously liked the POTUS account
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Some Facebook and Instagram users were surprised to find themselves automatically following the accounts of President Donald Trump or Vice President JD Vance this week after the president was inaugurated — but that's likely because they had previously followed official U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US GPU export limits could bring cold war to AI, data center markets
item.description: The US Biden administration, during its final week in office, issued a rule limiting sales of powerful AI chips to more than 100 countries, to keep the technology out of the hands of Chinese companies and the military there.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Sony’s surprising entry into electric vehicle market
item.description: Sony and Honda's Afeela 1 EV launches with advanced tech and entertainment for $89,900. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson gives his takeaways.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump, a populist president, is flanked by tech billionaires at his inauguration
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Some of the most exclusive seats at President Donald Trump’s inauguration on Monday were reserved for powerful tech CEOs who also happen to be among the world’s richest men.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump orders government not to infringe on Americans’ speech, calls for censorship investigation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump on Monday ordered that no federal officer, employee or agent may unconstitutionally abridge the free speech of any American citizen, an early step toward his campaign promise to dismantle what he called government “censorship” of U.S. citizens.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, ABC 4
